name: Plagiarism Self-Check & Similarity Audit
description: Multi-layer plagiarism risk audit: internal duplication, citation coverage, quote hygiene, source stability, and similarity pattern detection. Separates actual plagiarism risk from similarity noise.
ethical_boundary: Do not remove citations to reduce similarity. Do not hide source dependence. Do not invent sources. Do not damage citation integrity. The goal is academic integrity, not detector manipulation.

rules.description: Find near-duplicate paragraphs (>80% similarity) within the document. Common in theses where framework pillars are re-explained across chapters.
Count factual, non-opinion sentences and check if they have in-text citations. Patterns: (Author, Year), Author (Year), [N]
Find long quoted text (>40 words) and check: quotation marks present, immediate citation after, source traceable.
Check URLs and DOIs: still accessible, not from unstable platforms (blogs, social media, wikis), all factual claims point to stable sources.
Flag passages keeping same sentence order, concept sequence, distinctive phrases, examples, list structure as source. Must rewrite as synthesis.
When comparing to existing frameworks, always cite originals: McKinsey 7S, Kotter, TOGAF, NIST AI RMF, ISO/IEC 42001, OECD AI Principles, TAM, UTAUT, Dynamic Capabilities.
Detect text copied from researcher's own prior work without citation. Repeated definitions without cross-reference.

Classify similarity into legitimate citation/reference overlap, method terminology, internal repetition, long quote dependence, and suspicious close paraphrase. Report adjusted similarity instead of raw scare score.
Identify which chapter or section creates most plagiarism/similarity risk so improvement can be targeted without rewriting safe sections.

Verify every in-text citation (Author, Year) or [N] has a matching entry in the reference list, and every reference list entry appears in-text at least once. Orphan in-text citations signal uncited borrowed ideas. Orphan reference entries signal padding without use. Both are plagiarism indicators.

Flag DOIs that do not follow the standard 10.XXXX/... format, DOIs missing the https://doi.org/ prefix in the reference list, and DOIs where the prefix number has fewer than 4 digits. Malformed DOIs may indicate fabricated or inaccurately recorded sources. Do not mark missing DOIs as violations for books or technical reports where DOIs are uncommon.

turnitin_safe_synthesis.template:
Rewrite the following passage as academically safe synthesis. Do not remove citations.

Apply this structure:
1. State the research issue in thesis-specific language.
2. Group sources by claim instead of summarising one source at a time.
3. Explain the tension or gap between sources.
4. Connect to the researcher's own framework or argument.
5. Keep direct quotes only where necessary with proper attribution.

Passage:
{text}

Document type: {doc_type}
Formatting norm: {norm}

close_paraphrase_rewrite.template:
The following passage is too close to its source. Rewrite it as genuine synthesis:

{text}

Rules:
- Keep all citations
- Add researcher interpretation
- Add comparison with other sources
- Add limitation
- Change structure, not just wording
- Do not fabricate any source or data
